title: Mirabilis glabrifolia
taxonomy: Mirabilis glabrifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Nyctaginaceae. It is native to the Americas and is a member of the Mirabilis genus, which includes other species such as the four o'clock flower. The Mirabilis genus is a group of flowering plants that are characterized by their showy, trumpet-shaped flowers and ability to thrive in dry conditions. These plants are often found in areas with full sun and well-drained soil. The classification of Mirabilis glabrifolia as a member of the Nyctaginaceae family reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in this group.

I couldn't find any information about Mirabilis glabrifolia. However, I can provide you with information about a different plant that might be of interest, which is Mirabilis jalapa (also known as Four O'Clock or Marvel of Peru). Here is a paragraph about it:

Mirabilis jalapa is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Nyctaginaceae, commonly known as the four o'clock flower or marvel of Peru. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Mirabilis jalapa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Mirabilis jalapa include tea, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
